steps.title: Separate two sentences
steps.content: The first idea is complete at North American cinema. The next idea begins with Her film and forms a new independent clause about what the film is about, so the two ideas need a full sentence boundary.

steps.title: Avoid faulty joins
steps.content: Choice A leaves the clauses with no punctuation. Choice B creates an awkward and ungrammatical join with and her. Choice D uses only a comma between independent clauses. A period after cinema cleanly starts the second sentence. The answer is C.
